current.summary: Auditing and resolving gaps in the capmon information architecture design doc — worked through 5 of 14 identified items

activeWork.description: Completing the capmon IA design doc audit before implementation begins. 14 gaps were identified; 5 resolved this session, 9 remain.
activeWork.progress: Resolved items 1-5. Remaining items are 6-14 (listed in nextSteps). Design doc is significantly more complete. syllago-capmon-process skill also created as a side effect of item 4.

recentDecisions: canonical-keys.yaml: per-content-type structure; initial skills vocabulary derived from recognize.go + seeder specs (14 keys); capmon derive hard-rejects unknown keys
Graduation = adding a new canonical key, triggered when 2+ providers have the same concept in provider_extensions under different names
Workflow docs (update-format-doc.md, graduation-comparison.md) fully specified inline in design doc — extract verbatim to target files during implementation
capmon-process is a personal Claude Code skill (syllago-capmon-process), not part of the binary — created at $PAI_DIR/skills/syllago-capmon-process/
Loop invocation: cron job using claude -p, scheduled ~15min after CI action run; /loop 12h also valid but session-scoped
--local flag on capmon onboard: dropped. Use two commands instead: capmon onboard then claude -p /syllago-capmon-process
nextSteps: Item 6: Define .capmon-cache/<slug>/meta.json schema (fields: provider, content_type, source_uri, content_hash, fetched_at — but exact schema undefined)
Item 7: Clarify notes field in source manifests — machine-parsed trigger values (e.g. 'SPA' -> use Readability) or human-readable annotation for LLM?
Item 8: Define downstream effect of generation_method field (subagent | human-edited) — purely informational, or does pipeline behave differently?
Item 9: Structural diff in CI for source code — requires AST parsing (go/ast, ts-morph, etc). Is this proper AST diff or text diff acceptable for v1?
Item 10: Two .md URL patterns — what if both return 200? Take first, take longer, or always try both and merge?
Item 11: Provider removal — what happens to cache, open issues, format doc orphans when provider removed from providers.json?
Item 12: Loop failure / partial state — what is the recovery procedure if syllago-capmon-process fails mid-run?
Item 13: Migration period — does capmon check handle both *.md and *.yaml format docs during incremental migration?
Item 14: Diff preview size cap — GitHub issue body limit ~65536 chars; large source diffs could exceed this
Bonus: graduation_candidate: false backwards incentive — LLM sets own extensions to false by default; no re-evaluation path for existing extensions

context.notes: The design doc status header still says 'Design complete — panel-reviewed, ready for plan/implementation' — this should stay as-is until all 14 audit items are resolved and Holden decides the doc is truly ready. The syllago-capmon-process skill at $PAI_DIR/skills/syllago-capmon-process/ was created this session and is symlinked to ~/.claude/skills/. The skill's process-issues.md workflow references docs/workflows/update-format-doc.md and docs/workflows/graduation-comparison.md which are fully specified in the design doc but not yet extracted to their target files — that happens during implementation. Open Questions section of the design doc still has 3 items: Q1 (migration path, low stakes), Q2 (cache storage, lean stated), Q3 (loop polling, eliminated by cron decision). These should be resolved or explicitly closed as part of this audit.
